title: terebinthina rugosa
intro: Terebinthina rugosa, also known as Styrax tonkinensis, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the Styrax genus and characterized by its fragrant, white flowers and smooth, grayish-brown bark. The plant's resinous sap, also known as storax or benzoin, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Terebinthina rugosa include relief from coughs, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Terebinthina rugosa is often prepared as a resin, essential oil, or herbal tea, and is commonly used in aromatherapy, perfumery, and traditional folk medicine.
